mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and tighten financial conditions, which mechanically reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of high FEDFUNDS observations, or the Rank IC between FEDFUNDS levels and forward returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 30 basis points over a trailing three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and household consumption expectations, triggering broad de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the Rank IC is insignificant with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of declining real retail sales and an elevated federal funds rate creates binding liquidity constraints for consumer-dependent corporations, amplifying downside earnings revisions and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative RSAFS and high FEDFUNDS periods fail to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth out of a manufacturing trough reflects genuine order book recovery rather than inventory restocking, driving a sharp positive repricing of cyclical equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive from negative
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when INDPRO accelerates from trough levels, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over a 3-year window falls below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a high nominal federal funds rate sustains a restrictive real cost of capital, compressing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the interaction term carries a positive coefficient in a regression (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand that flows through to corporate revenue and earnings, producing consistent positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth is positive while S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below their trailing twelve-month average signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Trigger events coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return during triggers is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in industrial production and payroll employment indicates a broad-based production rebound rather than a temporary inventory fluctuation, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of joint INDPRO and PAYEMS acceleration fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the joint signal underperforms the single-series PAYEMS signal by more than 2 percentage points annualized.
